raw_consumer_statement: On XXXX, I initiated a dispute via XXXX, for a toll bridge charge of {$13.00} by XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX claimed that I crossed the bridge with my rental car on XXXX, even though I was nowhere near the bridge at the time. I tried to resolve this with XXXX first, but in vain. Therefore on XXXX, I disputed this charge via XXXX. In my dispute, I included details of my communication with XXXX, and I explained that the toll charge was a mistake because I never used the bridge. However, Citi closed my case within minutes, with the reason that "... Since the additional charge was incurred while the vehicle was in your possession, we consider it to be valid. '' Prior to this, I had never experienced credit card companies closing a dispute without even sending it to the merchant. Clearly, Citi didn't even read the information I included in my dispute. As far as I know, they had no rights acting like a judge in credit card disputes.
